      rw [h₅]
      exact h₄
    exact h₃

Alternatively, perhaps we can use that since `s` is `sSupIndep`, then for any finite subset `t ⊆ s`, `sSup t = Σ_{a ∈ t} a`. 

But in Lean, `iSupIndep` is defined as `∀ t : Finset ι, sSup (s '' t) = (t : Finset ι).sSup s`.

Therefore, to get `iSupIndep` of `(p.val c)`, we can try to use `sSupIndep` of `I`.

But Lean does not have an easy lemma for that. 

The best approach seems to be:

1. Prove that if `s : Set (PSubmod M)` is `sSupIndep`, and `f : (PSubmod M) → Submodule K (M c)` is order-preserving, then `(f p : p ∈ s)` is `iSupIndep`.
2. Apply this to `f = (p ↦ p c)` and `s = I`.

But perhaps we cannot do that. 

But perhaps we can use that for every `t : Finset I`, `sSup ( (fun p ↦ p.val) '' t ) = (t : Finset I).sSup (fun p ↦ p.val)` because `I` is `sSupIndep`.

But we need to prove that `sSup ( (fun p ↦ p.val c) '' t ) = (t : Finset I).sSup (fun p ↦ p.val c)`.

Since `p ↦ p.val c` is order-preserving and injective,

-- Prove iSupIndep
    have h₁ : iSupIndep fun p : I => (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
      -- Use the sSupIndep property from the direct sum decomposition
      have h₂ : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe I) := sSupIndep I
      -- Convert sSupIndep to iSupIndep
      have h₃ : iSupIndep fun p : I => (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
        -- Use the fact that the carrier set of I is the image of the canonical map
        -- and that sSupIndep implies iSupIndep
        convert IICS_iSupIndep (fun p : I => (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) (by simp [SetLike.ext_iff]) h₂ using 1
        <;> simp [SetLike.coe]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₃
    -- Convert the iSupIndep property to the required form
    convert h₁ using 1
    <;> simp [Submodule.coe_injective]
    <;> rfl
  · -- Prove iSup_eq_top
    have h₂ : (⨆ p : I, p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) = ⊤ := by
      -- Use the sSup_eq_top property from the direct sum decomposition
      have h₃ : sSup (SetLike.coe I) = ⊤ := sSup_eq_top I
      -- Convert sSup to iSup
      have h₄ : (⨆ p : I, p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) = sSup (SetLike.coe I) := by
        simp [sSup_eq_iSup]
        <;> simp_all [SetLike.coe]
        <;> aesop
      rw [h₄]
      <;> rw [h₃]
    -- Convert the iSup_eq_top property to the required form
    have h₃ : (⨆ p : I, p.val c : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⊤ := by
      have h₄ : (⨆ p : I, p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) = ⊤ := h₂
      have h₅ : (⨆ p : I, p.val c : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = (⨆ p : I, p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c := by
        apply congr_arg (fun p => p c)
        <;> simp [iSup_set, funext]
        <;> rfl
      rw [h₅]
      have h₆ : (⨆ p : I, p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) = ⊤ := h₄
      rw [h₆]
      <;> simp [Submodule.top']
    simpa [Submodule.coe_injective] using h₃

-- Prove that the supremum of the family is independent
    have h : iSupIndep (Set.range fun (p : I) => (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
      -- Use the fact that I is a direct sum decomposition to show that the family is independent
      have h₁ : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe I) := I.sSupIndep'
      -- Convert the supremum independence to infimum independence
      simpa [SetLike.coe] using h₁.infInf_independent
    -- Relate the infimum independence of the family to the iSupIndep condition
    have h₂ : iSupIndep fun p : I => (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
      -- Use the fact that the range of the function is the same as the set of submodules
      simpa [Set.range] using h
    -- Convert the iSupIndep condition to the required form
    simpa [Set.range] using h₂
  · -- Prove that the supremum of the family is the entire space
    have h : sSup (SetLike.coe I) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
    -- Use the fact that the supremum of the family is the entire space
    have h₁ : ⨆ p : I, (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) = ⊤ := by
      simpa [SetLike.coe] using h
    -- Convert the supremum to the required form
    have h₂ : ⨆ p : I, (p.val c : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⊤ := by
      -- Use the fact that the supremum of the family is the entire space
      simpa [iSup_psubtype] using congr_arg (fun p : Type _ => p c) h₁
    -- Relate the supremum to the required form
    simpa [iSup_psubtype] using h₂
